title: Heptapleurum angilogense
intro: Heptapleurum angilogense, also known as the Giant Schisandra or False Schisandra, is a type of climbing woody vine native to the tropical forests of Borneo. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and relieve symptoms of fever and pain. The bioactive constituents of Heptapleurum angilogense include lignans, schisandrins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory infections, colds, and flu.

The Araliaceae family is divided into several genera, with Heptapleurum being one of them. Heptapleurum angilogense is a specific species within the Heptapleurum genus. Its classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships to other members of the Araliaceae family.
